name: The Ashfields
description: Burned farmland three hours east of the gates. Something moved back in after the survivors left.

discoveryTable.label: A burned ledger page. Pre-Breach accounting records.

narrativeHook: The ash still smells of something that wasn't wood.

name: The Ruined Garrison
description: A military outpost from before the Breach. Four days north. Previous expeditions have not returned complete.

discoveryTable.label: Standing orders from before the Breach. Someone powerful issued them.

narrativeHook: The garrison flag is still flying. No one put it there recently.

name: The Green Corridor
description: A stretch of road south that still has trees. Two days. Relatively safe, relatively profitable.
narrativeHook: The traders used to call it the safe road. They don't anymore.

name: The Sunken Wharf
description: A drowned district below the harbor level. The flood came during the Breach and never fully receded. Good salvage. Very bad footing.

discoveryTable.label: A cargo manifest sealed in oilcloth. Pre-Breach trade routes, fully intact.

narrativeHook: The waterline marks on the buildings are twelve feet up. The water is still knee-deep in the lower streets.

name: The Breach Scar
description: The epicenter. Whatever opened during the Breach opened here. The ground is wrong. The mechanisms left behind are older than the city.

discoveryTable.label: Stone inscription in a language that predates Valdenmoor. The shapes feel like instructions.

narrativeHook: Three expeditions went in last month. Two came back. They don't talk about what they saw.

name: The Charnel Ravine
description: A deep gully east of the Mireward where plague bodies were disposed of during the Breach years. The Restored have been sending people here. Nobody knows exactly why.

discoveryTable.label: A Restored Order burial marker with a name that appears in living records.

narrativeHook: The smell is gone. Something replaced it.

name: The Toll Road Ruins
description: The southern trade route before the Corridor was cut. Bandits from the Ring control the tollhouses now. Clearing them is dangerous. Not clearing them is more expensive.

discoveryTable.label: A tollhouse ledger. Somebody important was paying to move something through here.

narrativeHook: The toll gates are still standing. Someone keeps them in good repair.

name: Merrow's Waypost
description: A contested transit point between the Northbank and Ash Quay. House Merrow claims it. The Compact contests that claim. The paperwork alone could fill a room.

discoveryTable.label: Transit logs with annotations from three different hands, each contradicting the last.

narrativeHook: Every faction in the city claims right-of-way here. Most of them are wrong.
